source_jobs.title: Principal Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
This role is ideally based in New York, however we’re open to considering applicants from other states and can discuss details during the application process.

At Unmind, we're here to create better mental health.

We believe mental health should be a source of strength, not struggle – for people, teams, and entire organisations.

We combine science, technology, and human care to help companies like Uber, Disney, Major League Baseball, and the NHS improve mental health and drive performance.

Now we’re looking for more brilliant people to help us do it.


🚀 OUR VALUES

We’re building a company where ambition and empathy go hand in hand.

Outpace the rest – Move fast. Deliver excellence.

Stronger together – We go further, faster, together.

Client first – We win, when our clients win.


🌈 INCLUSION & BELONGING

We’re building a place where everyone feels seen, heard, and supported – and where difference is celebrated.

If you need adjustments during the process, tell us when you apply or email talent@unmind.com.

Learn more about how we hire and our commitment to equity in our Candidate FAQ → https://www.notion.so/Unmind-Candidate-FAQs-b2f30c27bc57453b8edaee099aad4596?pvs=21


🎯 THE ROLE - PRINCIPAL ENTERPRISE ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE

Unmind is transforming workplace mental health, and we’re looking for an exceptional commercial leader to drive our largest and most complex enterprise deals.

This is a true top-tier enterprise sales role, selling directly to some of the largest organisations on the planet — including global brands like Disney, Uber and Standard Chartered — and closing seven-figure, board-level partnerships. You’ll operate as a trusted C-suite advisor, with rare access to CHROs and executive teams at the very top of the market, and work alongside Unmind’s most senior commercial leaders to close some of the biggest enterprise agreements in our history.

What you’ll do:

 - Partner with CEOs, CFOs, CHROs and Boards as a strategic advisor, shaping executive-level business cases grounded in financial and organisational impact — with genuinely exceptional access to senior decision-makers.

 - Originate, develop and close net-new enterprise opportunities, including global, multi-year agreements with some of the world’s most recognisable companies.

 - Lead highly complex sales cycles across multiple buying committees, geographies and functions (HR, Finance, Legal, IT, Risk).

 - Apply value-based, outcome-driven selling to close $1M–$5M+ ARR deals, with experience across $10M+ total contract value.

 - Influence Unmind’s enterprise go-to-market approach, mentoring senior sellers and raising the bar for how we win at the very top of the enterprise market.

This role is for a proven enterprise closer who thrives in ambiguity, brings sharp commercial judgement, and wants to sell a genuinely meaningful, Tech-for-Good product — shaping the future of workplace mental health at the highest level.


🌟 ABOUT YOU

In the spirit of equity and continuous growth, we invite you to remove education from your CV. Qualifications are not a factor in our decision-making. However, some experience in the following is important for this position:

 - Has proven success selling to C-suite and Board-level stakeholders in complex, enterprise B2B environments.

 - Brings 10+ years of enterprise sales experience, with clear evidence of:
   
   - Extensive experience selling to HR, People, Rewards, Benefits, or Wellbeing teams in large enterprises
   
   - 20+ seven-figure deals closed
   
   - Multiple eight-figure total contract value deals

 - Demonstrates exceptional business and financial acumen — able to speak credibly about ROI, operating models, productivity, risk, and enterprise transformation.

 - Is a hypothesis-led, consultative seller who challenges customer thinking and shapes strategy, rather than responding to RFPs or feature requests.

 - Has deep mastery of complex sales methodologies (e.g. MEDDPICC, Challenger, Value Selling) — and

source_jobs.description:
Location: UK Based, Remote-first (with monthly travel to London)



At Unmind, we're here to create better mental health.

We believe mental health should be a source of strength, not struggle – for people, teams, and entire organisations.

We combine science, technology, and human care to help companies like Uber, Disney, Major League Baseball, and the NHS improve mental health and drive performance.

🚀 Our Values

We’re building a company where ambition and empathy go hand in hand.

Outpace the rest – Move fast. Deliver excellence.

Stronger together – We go further, faster, together.

🎯 THE ROLE — PRODUCT MANAGER

We're looking for a Product Manager to own and evolve the Nova roadmap — our AI agent that supports employees with clinically grounded, meaningful interactions at the moments that matter most. You’ll work hands-on with prompt engineering and evaluation, partnering closely with clinical, data and engineering to improve quality, increase utilisation, and ship AI responsibly in a behavioural health context.

What you'll do

 - Own and deliver the Nova roadmap with support from a Principal Product Manager, translating goals into a clear, prioritised backlog.

 - Write and iterate prompts and conversation design, using strong product writing to improve clarity, empathy, and effectiveness.

 - Set up and run evaluation and testing practices for Nova (qualitative + quantitative), identifying failure modes and improving reliability.

 - Partner daily with clinical and data colleagues to ensure Nova is safe, clinically grounded, and continuously improving.

 - Define success metrics, analyse outcomes, and share learnings through clear updates and thoughtful post-mortems.




🌟 ABOUT YOU

In the spirit of equity and continuous growth, we invite you to remove education from your CV. Qualifications are not a factor in our decision-making. However, some experience in the following is important for this position:

 - Experience owning a backlog, defining success metrics, and shipping improvements iteratively in a product, product operations, technical project management, or similar role.

 - Background in healthcare or another regulated industry; you bring an appreciation for the rigor, governance, and stakeholder collaboration required to build products responsibly.

 - Hands-on experience working with LLM-powered products or conversational AI; you understand how prompts work, why they fail, and how to test them systematically.

 - Strong writing skills: you can write clearly, precisely, and empathetically — qualities that are as important for prompt design as they are for product communication.

 - Genuine curiosity about AI and its application in sensitive domains, with interest in the technical, ethical, and human dimensions of the work.

 - A data-driven working style: you define metrics before you ship, analyse results honestly, and write post-mortems without spin.

 - Comfort partnering closely with clinical or scientific colleagues: you respect domain expertise, ask good questions, and translate between clinical and product languages.

 - A serious attitude toward AI safety and clinical responsibility: you understand that Nova’s outputs have real consequences, and you design and test accordingly.

Tip for candidates: If you’re unsure whether to apply – do. We know great people don't always tick every box.




💚 LIFE AT UNMIND

We believe wellbeing and performance go hand-in-hand – and we build our environment to suppor

notes: Unmind has a strong focus on sales and account management, with only one product-focused role in the sample.
